tldr: Move from 100ms to Stream by re-architecting around Stream's chat/video/feeds APIs — note the product mix differs, so confirm Stream covers your specific use case before committing.
why_switch: Stream covers chat, activity feeds, and video in a single vendor — useful if you already need more than just rooms
Strong, broad SDK coverage across web and mobile platforms
US-headquartered vendor with mature enterprise tooling
what_to_export: Room/session IDs and configuration metadata from 100ms
Recordings and any stored media artifacts
Webhook endpoint definitions and subscribed events
Custom UI assets used in 100ms prebuilt components
User/account mappings if you plan to seed Stream users
API key inventory (for rotation, not reuse)
export_method: Pull room and session metadata via 100ms's official REST API and dashboard. Confirm recordings are intact in your storage bucket. Capture webhook configuration manually. Backend code (token mint, webhook handlers) is the canonical business logic and should be treated as the source of truth for the rebuild.
import_method: On Stream, provision an app, generate API keys, and implement server-side user provisioning and token issuance against Stream's official SDKs. Choose the relevant Stream product (Video, Chat, or Feeds) for each use case you are migrating. Swap the 100ms client SDK for Stream's per-platform SDK. Reconfigure webhooks to your handler using Stream's event schema and validate recording flows where applicable.

concept_mapping.note: Pick the matching Stream product for your use case; re-create entities fresh
Mint per user via Stream's server SDK; signing keys are vendor-specific
Stream has its own permission model; map by intent, not by name
Stream ships UI components per platform; expect to rebuild composition
Available in Stream Video; verify formats and storage destinations in their docs
Rewrite handler against Stream's event payloads

gotchas: Stream's product surface is broader (chat/feeds/video) — pick the right product per use case before scoping
Token formats are not portable; rewrite backend mint
If you depended on 100ms-specific room semantics, verify Stream's call lifecycle matches your assumptions
Mobile setup (permissions, native modules) must be re-validated
Pricing dimensions differ — confirm with Stream's official pricing page for your workload
Run both vendors in parallel during cutover to validate quality

email.subject: Migrating from 100ms to Stream
email.body:
Hi team,

I'm exploring a migration from 100ms to Stream. Stream covers chat, feeds, and video in one platform, which fits our roadmap if we plan to expand beyond plain video rooms.

Scope involves re-architecting room/session logic onto Stream's relevant product (Video for calls, Chat for messaging), rebuilding token issuance, swapping client SDKs, and reconfiguring webhooks. Estimating 2-4 weeks plus parallel-run time. I'll confirm exact features and pricing from Stream's official docs before locking the plan.

Let me know if you want to review.

Thanks
